title: Best crypto trading strategy for small capital ($500 or less)?
author: Witalson
selftext:
A lot of people start crypto trading with small capital and expect fast results.

From what I’ve seen, there are really only two ways to approach it:

* slow and low-risk growth
* or aggressive growth with higher risk

Most people naturally choose the second one.

  


# Leverage is usually the only way to scale fast

If you’re trading with \~$500, your position size is limited.

So many traders use leverage to increase exposure.

For example:

* $500 account
* strategy drawdown \~15%

Without leverage → manageable but slow

With leverage:

* 2x → \~30% drawdown
* 4x → \~60% drawdown

It’s not perfectly linear, but the idea is simple:

>

  


# The part people underestimate

Leverage introduces liquidation risk.

If the market moves against you:

* losses accelerate
* margin drops
* positions can get closed automatically

With small capital, that can hurt fast.

  


# So why do people still do it?

Because in the right conditions, it works.

Crypto markets can trend hard.

If you catch a good phase:

* returns scale quickly
* small accounts can grow much faster

I found that timing matters more than strategy sometimes.

For example, trending vs sideways markets make a huge difference:  
[https://getradiant.tech/updates/what-market-conditions-are-best-for-trading-strategies](https://getradiant.tech/updates/what-market-conditions-are-best-for-trading-strategies)

  


# Realistically

Yes, you *can* grow a small account 2–3x.

But:

* it’s not consistent
* it’s not safe
* and it involves real risk

  


# What seems more reasonable

Instead of going all-in:

* use moderate leverage (2–3x)
* don’t rely on one strategy
* accept drawdowns
* increase exposure only when market conditions are better

  


Curious how others here approach small accounts.

Do you go aggressive or play it safe?
